feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >I'm coming home from work >I avoid media full of propaganda >I'm going to relax…
entry.content: >I'm coming home from work >I avoid media full of propaganda >I'm going to relax by launching a game >Fentanyl man died >???????? The biggest psyop I've ever seen
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Vidya Art
entry.content: Anybody make some Vidya art, or really-- anything vidya related recently? For some reason I had a stroke of inspiration and decided to draw the Doom Marine taking a fat dump (yes, he IS sweating through the suit)
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: Just cracked my asscheeks open and whew boy does it smell terrible in there. Jus…
entry.content: Just cracked my asscheeks open and whew boy does it smell terrible in there. Just the filthiest, sulfuric, most eyewatering smell came billowing out. It's not pretty. A rotting carcass decaying in the Namib would be disgusted by the stench. Please remember to take baths regularly.
entry.dc:creator: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is OPENAI committing fraud? pic rel are the tokens that I've used recently over …
entry.content: Is OPENAI committing fraud? pic rel are the tokens that I've used recently over the past week with gpt 5.6 sol max for a rather small personal project which doesn't involve a lot of coding nor image/video tasks, mostly textual stuff (writing flash cards, extracted from local pdfs). 15.5 B tokens equals around(at 90% input 10% output ratio) The total cost comes out to around: $116,250 there is no way OpenAI isn't overcounting tokens... but why? Are you guys also experiencing similar overinflated
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: DCU slate update
entry.content: FILM >Superman Released, box office hit. >Supergirl Release, box office flop. >Clayface Completed. Releases October 23, 2026. >Man of Tomorrow Filming. Releases July 9, 2027. >Bane & Deathstroke In development, Matthew Orton (“Captain America: Brave New World”) writing the script. Slated to film in 2027. >The Brave and the Bold In development, Christina Hodson (“The Flash”) writing the script, Andy Muschietti (“The Flash”) to direct. >Wonder Woman In development, Ana Nogueira (“Supergirl”) writi
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: "Da Jews" is not an excuse for bad filmmaking
entry.content: I see a bunch of Nolanfags arguing that Nolan was forced by Hollywood Jewery to make an ugly film full of miscast trannies and outdated farm equipment. The reality is that he is just a tasteless Marvelfag status quo enjoyer like everyone else. Soviet filmmakers were working under the duress of being disappeared to Siberia and they made much more subversive films than modern Hollywood directors. Smart directors always find a way. Is reputational damage of being called a Chud worse than being thro
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Suikoden II as Mesozoic Animals
entry.content: Riou: Rhamphorhynchus Nanami: Shunosaurus Jowy: Protoceratops Luca Blight: Giganotosaurus Jillia Blight: Giganocephalus (A Fictional Species from Jurassic World: The Game) Agares Blight: Giganotosaurus Solon Jhee: Qianzhousaurus Culgan: Guanlong Seed: Pyroraptor Rowd: Kaprosuchus Han Cunningham: Irritator
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is Julia gonna make it?
entry.content: I tried Julia again for a week. Installing packages still takes forever. Importing still takes forever. The plotting performance is abysmal, it struggled with a million data points. I got errors trying and failing to install packages. The documentation us ok, but needs a technical writer to clean it up. I want to like Julia, I want a good Matlab replacement. It's definitely less buggy than the last time I tried it (around 1.0).
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: I’ve watched The Odyssey 11 times
entry.content: Hello everyone. George Carmi here, I’m the co-host of the @reeltokpodcast. As you can see by the title of this blog, I think I’m qualified enough to give my honest review of Christopher Nolan’s newest masterpiece. Here goes nothing: i wish i had something more meaningful to say right now, but i am at a major loss for words. several instances i found my body having a physical reaction to the pure filmmaking magic that i was experiencing. my eyes watered, my body began shaking, and i could feel an
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Do you really want me to believe that this is a conspiracy where 99,99999% peopl…
entry.content: Do you really want me to believe that this is a conspiracy where 99,99999% people are loving Odyssey, only /tv/ sees the truth?
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /b/ Random: I hate to admit when celebrity news actually affects me. In my defense, it isn't…
entry.content: I hate to admit when celebrity news actually affects me. In my defense, it isn't often. Last time was Trevor Moore dying, before that I can't even remember. These two splitting up makes me sad. Maybe I was naive, but their podcast did gain a special place in my heart because I thought "there are two people who REALLY like each other." I don't know if it's Tom losing the weight and getting rich, Christina getting cancer, or a combination, but just the fact that those are the likeliest culprits is
entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Does anyone watch this guy? https://www.youtube.com/@ShootingCa rs/videos He's d…
entry.content: Does anyone watch this guy? https://www.youtube.com/@ShootingCa rs/videos He's doing a small meet and greet in McHenry Illinois, with a cruise to the Volo Auto Museum. Info on the meet in this video https://youtu.be/fzpqlttkAZg I went to the first one they had a few years ago, it was pretty neat. Some cool folks showed up, but it was kinda small, maybe 20 cars. It was a good opportunity to do some networking with like-minded car folks. The Volo Auto Museum is pretty fucking great too. There will

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What is the point to owning one of these? >too fragile to move around >battery i…
entry.content: What is the point to owning one of these? >too fragile to move around >battery is literally unable to go on for more than 2 hrs so even if you move it, you'll always have to carry the massive brick around >sounds like a jet when you try to do even the most basic of tasks >you can't upgrade it so it's outdated within 2 months of use I literally don't get the appeal.

entry.content: /adhg/ Anthro Females Dominating Human Males General #1181 >”Prime slime time" edition This is a general dedicated to non-human female anthros (furries) dominating human men. Keep the discussion on-topic, and follow the theme of the general. (dominant women, subby males, etc.) >NO ERP >NO Cuckold/NTR >HIDE and IGNORE trolls --Stories-- To have your story listed in the OP, include STORYxUPDATE in your post. entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: just came back from imax, some thoughts people calling it woke are braindead, it…
entry.content: just came back from imax, some thoughts people calling it woke are braindead, its literally and metaphorically about return of patriarchy to chaos to bring it back to order. prestige and interstellar still better films. all scenes on the ship were absolutely kino, i felt like i was there. the best scene in my opinion was the sirens, it was very short, but incredibly directed, with surprising subtlety. the shot of cyclops closeup face while he's chewing a head if soldier and blood is dripping dow
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Looks like we lost...
entry.content: The backlash against Sony appears to be losing momentum. > Many PlayStation posts initially received 100,000+ protest replies > Recent posts are reportedly seeing closer to 10,000 > As time passes, it seems many players have started to move on For now, it looks increasingly unlikely that Sony will reverse its decision to phase out physical games.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >In Winston Groom's novel, Jenny is a much less central and less psychologically…
entry.content: >In Winston Groom's novel, Jenny is a much less central and less psychologically developed character than she is in the film. She is intelligent, idealistic, and independent, but she is also restless and unpredictable. Throughout the story, Jenny and Forrest have an on-and-off relationship. She genuinely cares about him at times, but she repeatedly leaves to pursue her own life, relationships, and ambitions. She studies, becomes involved in the counterculture of the 1960s, and later lives a bohe
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Social media platforms and shit
entry.content: Just to kill some time I've been eying what social platforms exists today. I'm just puzzled why there are so many different projects/platforms/protocols when they all have pretty much the same functionality, similar user interfaces and dozens of clients doing the same thing. Mastodon, Diaspora, Retroshare, Friendica, Matrix ... you name it. Freedom of choice is good for ya and shit, but too many similar platforms just spreads the potential user base. Why the devs of these can not collaborate and
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.content: If you are a rural south korean, your local municipality will pay you $14.5k to marry a Vietnamese woman and import her to your country so she can have kids with you. entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: esoteric empirically ethical existentialist druidic dyonisian disquietude
entry.content: All argumentation, then, is to be carried on either by induction or by deduction. Induction is a form of argument which leads the person with whom one is arguing to give assent to certain undisputed facts; through this assent it wins his approval of a doubtful proposition because this resembles the facts to which he has assented. For instance, in a dialogue by Aeschines Socraticus Socrates reveals that Aspasia reasoned thus with Xenophon's wife and with Xenophon himself: "Please tell me, madam,
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /news/ News: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed
entry.content: Despite a booming stock market and improving inflation numbers, the public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic and increasingly concerned about the cost of everyday goods, according to the latest CNBC All-America Economic Survey. The result: continued deeply negative approval numbers for President Donald Trump yet only a modest advantage for Democrats when it comes to the public’s preference for control of Congress. The survey of 1,000 adults
entry.dc:creator: /news/ News

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: At some point you just gotta admit Naziism was the biggest LARP in history, one …
entry.content: At some point you just gotta admit Naziism was the biggest LARP in history, one that too many people took too seriously. It wasn't some kind of people's movement or an attempt by the Germanic people to take back their country from the hands of greedy, moneygrabbing capitalists of Jewish ethnicity. In fact, they never killed any of those hyper-rich Jews like the Rothschilds or the Goldschmidts. Sure, they killed a bunch of middle-class Jewish people, including nuns, writers, musicians, shopkeeper
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Yesterday, you and your newfound companion found a handgun in an abandoned MAID …
entry.content: Yesterday, you and your newfound companion found a handgun in an abandoned MAID truck on the street. You've been carrying it with you ever since. As the two of you walk toward the town center, you come across an underground ventilation shaft and notice a young man, barefoot (pic related) kneeling at the bottom. He seems stuck, possibly hurt. He doesn't react to the two of you at all. You can't see a way out. Your companion grabs you by the arm and playfully suggests that you kill him to test out
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /int/ International: While I was walking the dog today I saw an african who looked like he'd just ste…
entry.content: While I was walking the dog today I saw an african who looked like he'd just stepped off the boat wearing his headcap, his flip-flops, his wifebeater and a crumbling backpack. He walked near me, spit on the ground and entered the local health center to enjoy his new free access to healthcare. Imagine being the doctor that has to treat him. I live in some random city in northern Spain. I frequently see africans walking around with backpacks when I go out now. I guess I'm gonna end up buying some
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Pretty much every artistic liberty Nolan took was kino. >Odysseus gets stuck on …
entry.content: Pretty much every artistic liberty Nolan took was kino. >Odysseus gets stuck on Calypso’s island because the lotus flowers suppressed his guilt. >Athena was a manifestation of his guilt over causing the downfall of Troy. >Sinon represents the innocent lives he had to sacrifice that he carried with him on his journey. >Helen is resentful about being used as propaganda to start this war. >Ex-Trojan War veterans like Odysseus were the Sea Peoples that caused the Bronze Age collapse. >The Odyssey is
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Let's face it: "AI" is going to be the most relevant technological topic in the …
entry.content: Let's face it: "AI" is going to be the most relevant technological topic in the coming years, but its fans make it impossible to have any intelligent discussions about it. Therefore any legitimate technology board will have to use "AI" to autoban "AI" fans.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Is this the most profound utterance by an actor ever?
entry.content: Long from now, when you are an old man, dying in your bed, your kin will ask you, "Where were you when Pedro Pascal said he wants to be on the right side of history?"
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Goddamn it EU
entry.content: You had ONE job, you fucktards. https://hackaday.com/2026/07/17/eu- adds-exemptions-to-user-serviceable -batteries-rules/ >muh wet devices Look at the fucking Galaxy S5! >if its battery retains at least 83% of its original capacity after 500 charge cycles, battery replacement has to be only replaceable by professionals. Why such a random number? How much $$$$ did apple give you? Fuck you, you spineless bastards.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /int/ International: Do you have these in your country? It's a rug washing station. it exists in ever…
entry.content: Do you have these in your country? It's a rug washing station. it exists in every city here, several of them per city. It's owned and run by the city as a service. You can go a clean your rugs there for free. (but you need to bring your own soap) Do you have this kind of infrastructure in your country/city?
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International
